States Cochrane Risk of Bias tool (RoB 2) was used to assess risk of bias.
Mentions meta-analyses performed using RevMan and R software, and GRADE for evidence certainty.
Reports 24 RCTs with 9634 participants meeting inclusion criteria.
Reports summary estimates with ORs, 95% CIs, and P-values for multiple outcomes including mortality, readmissions, medication adherence.
Mentions clinical effects affected by multiple aspects and more high-quality studies needed; GRADE ratings note low/very low quality evidence.

comparison_details.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of elastic stockings for prevention of thrombosis after orthopedic surgery.

items.ai_reason: Title is not shown in the provided text; the paper is described as meta-analysis but no explicit systematic review identification in title is visible.
Introduction describes DVT, current preventive measures, and explains that evidence on elastic stockings remains unclear, justifying the review.
Objective explicitly stated: to investigate the preventive effect of elastic stockings on DVT after orthopedic surgery via meta-analysis.
Detailed inclusion and exclusion criteria are provided, including study type, population, intervention, and outcome definitions.
PubMed, Embase, and Cochrane are listed, but the date when each was last searched is not specified.
Only keywords provided; no full search strategy with Boolean operators, filters, or complete query string for any database.
States two researchers searched databases but does not specify whether screening was independent or how disagreements were resolved.
Mentions researchers read full texts and extracted data but does not describe independent duplicate extraction or disagreement resolution.
Outcome specified: incidence of DVT, diagnosed by biphasic ultrasound.
Lists extracted variables: authors, year, number, sex ratio, age, surgical procedure, intervention methods.
States Cochrane criteria used across six domains but does not indicate how many reviewers assessed risk of bias or whether independently.
Effect measure specified as odds ratio (OR) with 95% CI.
Describes subgroup grouping by surgery type (ankle vs. hip/knee arthroplasty) after sensitivity exclusion.
States that percentages were converted to actual case numbers for analysis.
Describes use of forest plots and funnel plots, with data tabulated.
Describes Mantel-Haenszel method, fixed/random effect models based on heterogeneity, I² and Q tests, using Revman 5.3.5.
Subgroup analysis by surgical procedure described as approach to explore heterogeneity.
Sensitivity analysis by case-by-case exclusion method described.
Funnel plot described as method to assess publication bias.
No method such as GRADE used to assess certainty of the body of evidence.
States 90 studies initially screened, 7 included, and references Figure 1 flow chart.
No list or citations of studies excluded at full-text review with reasons.
Table 1 presents baseline characteristics of each included study with citations.
Table 2 presents risk of bias assessment across six domains for each study.
Table 1 presents DVT events per group and forest plots display effect estimates with CIs per study.
Subgroup characteristics summarized in results and discussion by surgery type.
Pooled OR with 95% CI, Z values, P values and I² heterogeneity reported for overall and subgroup analyses.
Subgroup analysis results reported showing heterogeneity between ankle vs. hip/knee subgroups (I²=70.4%).
Results of sensitivity analysis reported, showing exclusion of Takahashi study resulted in homogeneity (I²=0%, P=0.46).
Funnel plot results presented, showing asymmetry suggesting publication bias.
No assessment of certainty of evidence (e.g., GRADE) reported.
Discussion interprets results relative to prior meta-analyses by Sachdeva et al. and Hajibandeh et al.
Discusses limitations such as different stocking types and variable intervention duration across studies.
Acknowledges review did not distinguish stocking types or record intervention duration.
Discusses implications for practice (combining with anticoagulants) and clinical recommendations.
No registration information provided nor statement that the review was not registered.
No mention of protocol accessibility or whether a protocol was prepared.
No information about amendments to protocol provided.
Funding statement explicitly states 'Funding: None.'
Authors declare no conflicts of interest via ICMJE form.
No mention of availability of data collection forms, extracted data, analytic code, or other materials.
Title explicitly identifies the report as 'A systematic review and meta-analysis'.
The abstract states the objective: to investigate the preventive effect of elastic stockings on DVT after orthopedic surgery.
Only mentions RCTs published from 2008; does not specify detailed inclusion/exclusion criteria such as population, intervention, or outcomes.
Specifies PubMed, Embase and Cochrane as databases, with a publication range from 2008 to date, though exact search date is not given.
No mention of any method used to assess risk of bias in included studies.
Describes use of Revman 5.3.5, random/fixed effects models, forest/funnel plots, heterogeneity testing, sensitivity and subgroup analyses.
Reports 7 included studies with 3,116 patients, and mentions subgroup characteristics (ankle surgery vs hip/knee arthroplasty).
Reports pooled OR=0.59 (95% CI 0.34-1.03) and subgroup results with Z and P values, indicating direction of effect favoring stockings in ankle surgery.
No discussion of limitations of the evidence such as risk of bias, inconsistency, or imprecision.
Provides interpretation: elastic stockings effective in ankle surgery but not hip/knee/spine; recommends combining with anticoagulants and other physical therapies.
No funding source is mentioned in the abstract.
No registration name or number is provided in the abstract.
